description: Auto-generated JSON Schema for Splice PlanData types. Generated from frontend/src/interfaces/planInterfaces.ts

nodePagePositions.description: Per-page node positions (layout mode). Falls back to node.position when absent.

nodePageSizes.description: Per-page node sizes (layout mode). Falls back to node.size when absent.

linkPageGeometry.description: Per-page link geometry overrides (waypoints, pathStyle, endpoints). Falls back to link properties when absent.

nodePageHideEmptyPins.description: Per-page hide-empty-pins overrides. Falls back to node.hideEmptyPins when absent. nodeId → pageId → boolean

defaultSchematicDisplayMode.description: Default display mode for newly created conductors in schematic view. undefined = Wire.

PlanData.description:
PlanData - The Core Data Structure

This is the root data structure containing all plan elements. Uses a **flat model** where conductors, wire groups, and cables are top-level entities, not nested within links.

## Structure Overview

``` PlanData  ├─ nodes      // Components and branch points  ├─ links      // Bundle connections between nodes  ├─ conductors // Wire entities with all properties  ├─ conductorSplices // Junctions at branch points  ├─ wireGroups // Twisted/braided/taped bundles  ├─ cables     // Multi-conductor cables  ├─ signals    // Signal definitions  ├─ mates      // Connector mate relationships  ├─ deviceGroups // Device groupings  └─ bom        // Bill of Materials ```

## Flat Model Benefits

1. Single source of truth for wire properties (directly on PlanConductor) 2. Explicit connectivity via ConductorSplice (no implicit routing) 3. Easy to query conductors by link, by pin, or by properties 4. Clean separation of geometry (links) and routing (conductors)

PlanNode.description:
PlanNode - Components and Branch Points

Nodes represent physical elements in the harness topology.

## Node Types

- **component**: Connectors, fuses, relays, etc. with pins
- **branch_point**: Junction where conductors meet/branch

## Component Pins

Components have pins that conductors connect to: ```typescript pins: [   { id: "pin_1", label: "1" },   { id: "pin_2", label: "2", signalId: "sig_power" } ] ```

## Branch Point Routing

Branch points have routing data defining conductor connections: ```typescript routing: {   spliceNodes: [     { id: "sn1", conductorIds: ["cond1", "cond2"] }   ] } ```

PinLead.description: Per-pin lead data for pigtail components. A pigtail component ships with pre-attached wires on some or all pins. Colors are hex (plan-model convention). Convert via hexToColorName() for BOM.

EndpointTermination.description: Per-endpoint termination specification — how a wire connects at one end

ContactMethod.description: Method used to terminate a wire at a connector pin

spliceNodes.description: Splice nodes - groups of conductors that connect at this branch point.
spliceNodes.deprecated:
Use data.conductorSplices instead. The new model stores splices
at the top level (data.conductorSplices) rather than on individual branch points.

BranchPointRouting.description:
BranchPointRouting - Routing Data for Branch Points

Stored on branch point nodes as `node.routing`. SpliceNodes define electrical connectivity between conductors at this branch point.

## Required for All Junctions

Every conductor junction REQUIRES a SpliceNode entry. There is no implicit continuity - all connections must be explicit.

## Example

```typescript branchPoint.routing = {   spliceNodes: [     // Continuity: same wire passing through     { id: "sn1", conductorIds: ["cond1", "cond2"] },     // Splice: different wires joined     { id: "sn2", conductorIds: ["gnd1", "gnd2"], spliceType: "butt" }   ] } ```

SpliceNode.description:
SpliceNode - Electrical Connection Between Conductors

A splice node represents electrical connectivity between conductors at a branch point. SpliceNodes are REQUIRED for ALL conductor junctions (no implicit continuity).

## Key Distinction: Pass-through vs Splice

| spliceType    | Meaning                                          | |---------------|--------------------------------------------------| | undefined     | Continuity - same wire passing through (no part) | | 'butt', etc.  | Actual splice - physical connection with part    |

## Pass-through Example (one wire, multi-link)

```typescript // Same wire continues through branch point (no physical splice) spliceNode: {   id: "sn_1",   conductorIds: ["cond1", "cond2"],  // Same logical wire   // spliceType: undefined = continuity } ```

## Splice Example (different wires joined)

```typescript // Two different wires electrically connected spliceNode: {   id: "sn_2",   conductorIds: ["gnd_1", "gnd_2", "gnd_3"],  // Different wires   spliceType: "butt",   splicePartName: "Butt Splice 18-22 AWG" } ```

SpliceNodes can connect conductors from DIFFERENT ConductorLinks (different nets).
SpliceNode.deprecated:
Use ConductorSplice instead. SpliceNodes are the old model stored on
node.routing.spliceNodes. The new model uses ConductorSplice stored in data.conductorSplices.
In the new model, pass-through is modeled by conductor.linkPath spanning multiple links
(no splice entry needed), while ConductorSplice always represents an actual electrical connection.

PlanLink.description:
PlanLink - Bundle Between Nodes

A link represents the physical cable bundle connecting two nodes.

## Flat Model Architecture

With the flat conductor model, conductors are top-level entities. Links only contain geometry/rendering information, NOT conductors.

To find conductors on a link: ```typescript const conductors = Object.values(data.conductors)   .filter(c => c.linkId === link.id); ```

## Bundle Properties

Links can specify physical properties like length and tolerances: ```typescript {   length_mm: 500,   length_tolerance_mm: 25  // +/- 25mm } ```

distance.description: Distance along the cardinal exit direction before bending (default: 20px)

radius.description: Curve radius at the bend (0 = sharp corner, larger = smoother). Default: 12

PlanLinkBendPoint.description: Configurable bend point at a component exit

PlanLinkAccessory.description:
PlanLinkAccessory - Bundle Covering/Accessory on a Plan Link

Mirrors the assembly-level BundleAccessory but uses plan-level addressing. Supports heat shrink, sleeves, boots, cable ties, tape, grommets, etc.

## Example

```typescript {   id: "acc_123",   type: "heat_shrink",   position: 0.2,        // 20% along bundle   length: 0.1,          // covers 10% of bundle   partNumber: "HS-3/16",   manufacturer: "Raychem" } ```

PlanConductor.description:
PlanConductor - Unified Wire with All Properties

A conductor represents a complete wire with exactly 2 endpoints. All wire properties (gauge, color, etc.) are stored directly on the conductor.

## Key Differences from PlanConductor (legacy)

1. **Self-contained**: All properties on conductor (no ConductorLink indirection) 2. **Explicit endpoints**: startEndpoint/endEndpoint instead of startPinId/endPinId 3. **Route tracking**: linkPath array shows path through topology 4. **Always 2 endpoints**: No multi-link conductors; use ConductorSplice for junctions

## Example: Wire from connector to connector

```typescript {   id: "cond_123",   netName: "CAN_H",   gauge: "18 AWG",   color: "#00FF00",   startEndpoint: { nodeId: "x1", pinId: "pin_1" },   endEndpoint: { nodeId: "x2", pinId: "pin_3" },   linkPath: ["link_1", "link_2", "link_3"]  // Route through topology } ```

## Example: Wire ending at branch point (for splice)

```typescript {   id: "cond_456",   netName: "GND",   gauge: "16 AWG",   color: "#000000",   startEndpoint: { nodeId: "x1", pinId: "pin_gnd" },   endEndpoint: { nodeId: "bp1" },  // No pinId = branch point   linkPath: ["link_1"] } ```

When conductors meet at a branch point, use ConductorSplice to define their electrical relationship (continuity or splice).

ConductorEndpoint.description:
ConductorEndpoint - Where a conductor terminates

A conductor always has exactly 2 endpoints. Each endpoint is either: 1. A pin on a component (nodeId + pinId) 2. A branch point node (nodeId only, no pinId)

## Examples

Pin on component: ```typescript { nodeId: "x1", pinId: "pin_1" } ```

Branch point (for splice/continuity): ```typescript { nodeId: "bp1" }  // No pinId = branch point ```

ConductorSplice.description:
ConductorSplice represents an electrical connection between conductors at a branch point.

IMPORTANT: ConductorSplice always represents an ACTUAL splice (electrical connection). Pass-through (no splice) is modeled by a conductor with multi-link linkPath - no splice entry.

Best practice: Always set spliceType or splicePartId to indicate the splice method.

PlanWireGroup.description:
PlanWireGroup - Bundled Conductors

Groups conductors that are physically bundled together (twisted, braided, or taped).

## Single Link Constraint

Wire groups CANNOT branch - they exist within a single link only. If a twisted pair continues through a branch point, each link segment has its own WireGroup referencing the conductors in that segment.

## Example

```typescript {   id: "wg_123",   linkId: "link_1",   name: "Twisted Pair A",   method: "twisted",   conductorIds: ["cond_can_h", "cond_can_l"],   twistPitchMm: 25 } ```

**Note**: `conductorIds` are **references** to top-level conductors, not duplicated data.

PlanCable.description:
PlanCable - Multi-Conductor Cables

Represents a cable assembly with multiple cores mapped to conductors.

## Single Link Constraint

Cables CANNOT branch - they exist within a single link only. If a cable continues through a branch point, each link segment has its own PlanCable referencing the conductors in that segment.

## Example

```typescript {   id: "cable_123",   linkId: "link_1",   cableName: "4-conductor shielded",   cablePartId: "alpha_5099c",   coreMappings: [     { coreNumber: 1, conductorId: "cond_power" },     { coreNumber: 2, conductorId: "cond_ground" },     { coreNumber: 3, conductorId: "cond_can_h" },     { coreNumber: 4, conductorId: "cond_can_l" }   ] } ```

CableCoreMapping.description:
Maps a cable core to a conductor.

Each core in a multi-conductor cable is mapped to a top-level conductor.

PlanSignalDefinition.description:
PlanSignalDefinition - User-Definable Signal Types

Signals define the logical purpose of a wire (power, ground, CAN, etc.) and can provide default wire properties.

## Usage

Signals are referenced by ConductorLinks via `signalId`: ```typescript conductorLink.signalId = "sig_power";  // References signals["sig_power"] ```

## Example

```typescript {   id: "sig_power",   name: "Power Supply",   color: "#FF0000",   description: "12V power distribution",   defaultGauge: 18,   defaultWireColor: "#FF0000",   defaultVoltage: 12 } ```

PlanNet.description:
PlanNet - Named Net with Signal Reference

Nets are the first-class entity that owns signal classification. Conductors reference nets via `netName` (an opaque internal key — never shown to users).

## Display Name Resolution (via `useNetDisplay` composable)

1. `PlanNet.displayName` — user-edited override (set in Nets panel) 2. Auto-generated from signal + endpoints: `{signalLabel}_{node.pin}` 3. Fallback: `Net {stableIndex}`

All conductors in the same unified net group share the same display name.

## Example

```typescript {   name: "NET_abc123",      // opaque internal key   signalId: "sig_can",   displayName: "CAN_H",    // user-facing override   description: "CAN High bus",   color: "#00FF00" } ```

assemblyLinks.description: Multi-link assembly topology (3+ endpoints or complex topologies). Each entry describes one link segment between two endpoint indices.

BomLeadSpec.description: BOM-level lead specification. Uses color NAMES (BomEntrySpec convention), not hex. Conversion from PinLead hex → BomLeadSpec name via hexToColorName() at BOM generation.

AssemblyCoreSpec.description: Per-core specification in an assembly cable

AssemblyCoreTermination.description: Contact/termination at one end of a core

AssemblyWireGroupSpec.description: Wire group (twisted pair, etc.) by core indices

AssemblyLinkSpec.description: A link segment between two endpoints in a multi-link assembly topology

VisualGroup.description: Represents a user-drawn rectangular frame that can contain nodes. Nodes inside the bounds are automatically members (with manual override). Supports nesting (groups inside groups).

PlanPage.description: A page in the plan view. Layout and Schematic modes have independent pages.

PlanAssemblyRef.description: Reference to an off-the-shelf cable assembly placed in the plan. Elements tagged with assemblyRefId are structurally locked.

SubassemblyInstance.description: A subassembly instance groups plan elements that were inserted together from a saved fragment (subassembly template). Unlike PlanAssemblyRef which represents an off-the-shelf purchased assembly, SubassemblyInstance tracks a logically related set of nodes/links/conductors inserted as a unit.

PlanViewStateData.description: Persistable subset of plan view state (saved with plan data to backend)

PlanLink - Bundle Between Nodes

A link represents the physical cable bundle connecting two nodes.

## Flat Model Architecture

## Bundle Properties

Links can specify physical properties like length and tolerances: ```typescript {   length_mm: 500,   length_tolerance_mm: 25  // +/- 25mm } ```

distance.description: Distance along the cardinal exit direction before bending (default: 20px)

radius.description: Curve radius at the bend (0 = sharp corner, larger = smoother). Default: 12

A conductor represents a complete wire with exactly 2 endpoints. All wire properties (gauge, color, etc.) are stored directly on the conductor.

## Example: Wire from connector to connector

All conductors in the same unified net group share the same display name.

AssemblyCoreSpec.description: Per-core specification in an assembly cable

AssemblyCoreTermination.description: Contact/termination at one end of a core

ContactMethod.description: Method used to terminate a wire at a connector pin

AssemblyWireGroupSpec.description: Wire group (twisted pair, etc.) by core indices

AssemblyLinkSpec.description: A link segment between two endpoints in a multi-link assembly topology

PlanLinkAccessory.description:
PlanLinkAccessory - Bundle Covering/Accessory on a Plan Link

Mirrors the assembly-level BundleAccessory but uses plan-level addressing. Supports heat shrink, sleeves, boots, cable ties, tape, grommets, etc.

## Example

```typescript {   id: "acc_123",   type: "heat_shrink",   position: 0.2,        // 20% along bundle   length: 0.1,          // covers 10% of bundle   partNumber: "HS-3/16",   manufacturer: "Raychem" } ```

PlanAssemblyRef.description: Reference to an off-the-shelf cable assembly placed in the plan. Elements tagged with assemblyRefId are structurally locked.

VisualGroup.description: Represents a user-drawn rectangular frame that can contain nodes. Nodes inside the bounds are automatically members (with manual override). Supports nesting (groups inside groups).

connector_side.description: Which end of bundle for strip length.

edgeCurveAnchors.description: Per-edge curve anchors (keyed by edge ID)
edgeCurveAnchors.deprecated:
Use edgeBezierPaths instead. This legacy 3-zone system provides
limited control compared to full Bezier path editing. Existing curve anchors
will be auto-migrated to Bezier paths when the edge is first edited.

Allows manual reshaping of curved cables/bundles by dragging different zones.
Each zone affects different parts of the curve:
- source: near the source connector (t ≈ 0.25)
- middle: center of the curve (t ≈ 0.5)
- target: near the target connector (t ≈ 0.75)

AnchorPointType.description: Type of anchor point for bezier curves.

hasShield.deprecated: Shield is now auto-detected from cable spec

showLegend.deprecated: Legend is now always shown for cables

BundleSegment.description:
Bundle segment representing a physical routing path between two nodes. This is the PRIMARY routing primitive - derived from plan links.

A segment contains all wires and cables that pass through this path section. Cables/wires spanning multiple segments create edges for each segment.

WireGroupMetadata.description: Wire group metadata within a bundle segment. Represents user-defined groupings like twisted pairs, braided wires, etc.

ExplicitBundle.deprecated: Use BundleSegment instead. Kept for backward compatibility loading.

connector_side.description: Which end of bundle for strip length.

edgeCurveAnchors.description: Per-edge curve anchors (keyed by edge ID)
edgeCurveAnchors.deprecated:
Use edgeBezierPaths instead. This legacy 3-zone system provides
limited control compared to full Bezier path editing. Existing curve anchors
will be auto-migrated to Bezier paths when the edge is first edited.

Allows manual reshaping of curved cables/bundles by dragging different zones.
Each zone affects different parts of the curve:
- source: near the source connector (t ≈ 0.25)
- middle: center of the curve (t ≈ 0.5)
- target: near the target connector (t ≈ 0.75)

AnchorPointType.description: Type of anchor point for bezier curves.

hasShield.deprecated: Shield is now auto-detected from cable spec

showLegend.deprecated: Legend is now always shown for cables

BundleSegment.description:
Bundle segment representing a physical routing path between two nodes. This is the PRIMARY routing primitive - derived from plan links.

A segment contains all wires and cables that pass through this path section. Cables/wires spanning multiple segments create edges for each segment.

WireGroupMetadata.description: Wire group metadata within a bundle segment. Represents user-defined groupings like twisted pairs, braided wires, etc.

ExplicitBundle.deprecated: Use BundleSegment instead. Kept for backward compatibility loading.
